overview.description: Agentic OS for enterprise AI agent governance and observability
overview.description_long: Complexity is an Agentic OS that enables enterprises to define Behavior Contracts for AI agents, monitor every agent action in real time, govern actions via approve/block/kill controls, and report governance posture via a single Governance Score. Originally positioned as a software 3.0 platform for codifying knowledge-worker tasks into intelligent automatable processes using natural language, the product has evolved into a full AI-agent governance layer supporting LangChain, CrewAI, Copilot Studio, Agentforce, and custom agents. Addresses CISO, finance, compliance, and IT operations personas with a visual, non-technical interface and EU AI Act Article 14 audit packages.
overview.tagline: The Operating System for Your AI Agents

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague with mission to transform organizations into intelligent decision-making systems by codifying knowledge-worker reasoning into automatable AI processes.

key_milestones.event: Launched V12 of Agentic OS with full five-layer governance (Define, Observe, Analyze, Act, Report)

metadata.notes: Very early-stage startup founded 2025 with 2-10 employees. Company is listed under parent 'Hyperspace' on CNAIP but identity of that parent entity is unclear. Website complexity.dev shows a mature product (V12) for enterprise AI agent governance. Pricing: free tier (3 agents), Team $499/month, Enterprise custom. Main differentiator: 5-layer governance in one visual platform (vs competitors who cover only 1-2 layers).

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Works in commoditizing space (generative_ai, automation) but has some differentiation through proprietary tech or customer base.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Small team with no visible customer traction or market validation signals.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Acquired by Hyperspace — funded by parent entity.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Multiple high-risk factors across AGI displacement, market, or funding dimensions.
